etymology.description: semantic 宀 (roof) + phonetic 元 (sound) -> finished; intact
etymology.interpretation: The character 完 (wán, "finished; intact") combines the semantic element 宀 (mián, "roof"), suggesting a protective or structural component, and the phonetic element 元 (yuán, "origin"), indicating the pronunciation. Together, they imply a structure that is completed and covered, hence the sense of something being "finished" or "intact."

examples.chinese: 他终于完成任务了(他終於完成任務了)。
晚餐做好了(晚餐做好了)，可以开饭了(可以開飯了)。
examples.pinyin: Wǎncān zuò hǎo le, kěyǐ kāifàn le.
examples.english: Dinner is finished and ready to be served.

examples.chinese: 我的工作还没完(我的工作還沒完)，需要加班(需要加班)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ de gōngzuò hái méi wán, xūyào jiābān.
examples.english: My work is not finished yet and I need to work overtime.
